journal_entry.description: Digital marketing campaign for snack products
journal_entry.rationale: Cannot determine the correct entry because: (1) The province/territory is unknown, which determines the applicable tax rate (HST 13-15% in ON/NB/NL/NS/PE; GST 5% + PST in BC/SK/MB; GST 5% only in AB/NT/NU/YT; GST 5% + QST 9.975% in QC). (2) Need confirmation whether this digital marketing service is taxable. (3) Without knowing the tax treatment, cannot split the $22,000 into base amount and tax components, which affects both the expense amount and whether to record HST/GST Receivable.
clarification_questions: Which province or territory is Mondelez operating in for this transaction?
Is this digital marketing campaign considered a taxable supply under Canadian tax law (subject to GST/HST)?

output.reason: Marketing campaign = expense increase. Mondelez paid for advertising/marketing services, which is an operating expense. The debit increases the marketing/advertising expense account (slot c = expense increase).

debit_classifier.reason: Marketing campaign = expense increase. Mondelez paid for advertising/marketing services, which is an operating expense. The debit increases the marketing/advertising expense account (slot c = expense increase).

output.reason: Cash payment of $22,000 = asset decrease. Mondelez is paying cash out of the business, which decreases the Cash asset account.

credit_classifier.reason: Cash payment of $22,000 = asset decrease. Mondelez is paying cash out of the business, which decreases the Cash asset account.
